description_long: This object is a classic record player with a rectangular wooden base featuring a rich, dark wood grain texture. The top surface houses a large, circular black platter at the center, designed to hold vinyl records. To one side, a slender tonearm with a cylindrical counterweight and a pivoting mechanism is visible, extending toward the platter. A small, rectangular control area is present on one corner, likely for power or speed adjustment, and is colored in a contrasting gray. The overall design is minimalistic, with clean lines and a vintage aesthetic. No dust cover or additional buttons are visible, emphasizing the simplicity and elegance of the device.
description: A vintage-style record player with a dark wooden base, black platter, and a sleek tonearm. Minimal controls and a clean, classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular black platter centered on a dark wooden base, with the tonearm and control area visible on the right.
description_view_1: Opposite side view highlighting the platter and the tonearm now on the left, with the control area more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the tonearm resting across the platter and the control area on the left.
description_view_3: Side view with the tonearm and control area on the left, and the platter dominating the center.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, large, and circular, occupying the central area of the record player. It is black in color, appears to be made of plastic or metal, and has a smooth, matte finish with no visible grooves or markings. The platter is slightly raised above the wooden base and is proportionally dominant compared to the rest of the device.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, slender, and elongated, extending from one corner of the base toward the platter. It is primarily black with metallic elements, has a cylindrical counterweight at the end, and appears to be made of metal and plastic. The tonearm is resting in its holder, not positioned over the platter.
